summary: PR #2466 resplit the former Popo ftPp_SpecialS translation-unit range into ftPp_SpecialS.c, new ftPp_SpecialHi.c, and new ftPp_SpecialLw.c, updated splits/symbol metadata, linked the three Popo objects as Matching in configure.py, and adjusted includes/header surfaces for callers. Bot output reported +14416 linked-code bytes and 80 new matches, with corresponding broken matches in the old ftPp_SpecialS unit caused by the resplit. The slice includes one author review comment calling out a deliberately added sdata2_ordering float-alignment hack in ftPp_SpecialHi.c.

key_files.role: Original Popo SpecialS source was reduced after moving later address ranges into new SpecialHi and SpecialLw files.
New matched source file for the 0x80120E68-0x80122898 split, including SpecialHi functions and some ftPp_SpecialS_* functions now located in this unit.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists many 100% new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialHi

key_files.role: New matched source file for the 0x80122898-0x80122EDC split.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialLw

key_files.role: Adjusted translation-unit section boundaries for ftPp_SpecialS and added split entries for ftPp_SpecialHi and ftPp_SpecialLw.
Changed several Popo-adjacent data and sdata2 symbols from global address-style names to local @ labels with explicit sizes, alignments, and data kinds.
Changed ftPp_SpecialS.c from NonMatching to Matching and added Matching objects for ftPp_SpecialHi.c and ftPp_SpecialLw.c.
Header surface updated for SpecialHi declarations after the resplit.
New header for SpecialLw declarations after the resplit.
Header declarations removed as responsibilities moved to the new split headers.
Include updated from old ftPp_1211.h to new SpecialHi/SpecialLw headers.
Includes updated to reference new Popo SpecialHi and SpecialLw headers.
Includes updated to reference new Popo SpecialHi and SpecialLw headers.
Small ftdata include/reference adjustment listed in changed-file metadata; exact hunk not present in loaded diff excerpt.

what_changed: Resplit ftPp_SpecialS.c so its text section ends at 0x80120E68 instead of 0x80122EDC.
Added ftPp_SpecialHi.c covering text 0x80120E68-0x80122898 and sdata2 0x804D9860-0x804D9890.
Added ftPp_SpecialLw.c covering text 0x80122898-0x80122EDC and sdata2 0x804D9890-0x804D9898.
Marked ftPp_SpecialS.c, ftPp_SpecialHi.c, and ftPp_SpecialLw.c as Matching objects in configure.py.
Replaced old ftPp_1211.h includes in touched callers with ftPp_SpecialHi.h and ftPp_SpecialLw.h.
Changed Popo-adjacent data/sdata/sdata2 symbol ownership from global address-style names to local @ labels in symbols.txt.
Bot report recorded 80 new matches and 77 broken matches, where the broken matches are shown under the old ftPp_SpecialS unit and correspond to functions now reported under the new units.

smart_moves.text: The PR paired source resplitting with splits.txt boundaries and configure.py object status changes, making the new SpecialHi/SpecialLw units explicit rather than only moving code.
Include users were moved away from an old aggregate/unknown header name toward the new split-specific headers.
The author explicitly flagged the sdata2_ordering helper as a hack rather than presenting it as normal style.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a historical object was over-broad, a resplit can convert apparent regressions in the old unit into new matches in the correct new units; read bot broken-match tables alongside split-boundary changes before treating them as true regressions.
evidence_refs: diff:splits.txt ftPp_SpecialS shortened and ftPp_SpecialHi/Lw added
text_corpus:80 new matches and 77 broken matches listed under old/new units

decomp_lessons.lesson: Header cleanup should accompany a resplit when external users need declarations from the newly separated files.

naming_conventions.text: The resplit uses move-specific file/header names ftPp_SpecialHi and ftPp_SpecialLw instead of the old ftPp_1211 include name.
Unproven data symbols in the touched range were represented as local @ labels rather than semantic global names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used splits.txt section boundaries to carve one large SpecialS range into SpecialS, SpecialHi, and SpecialLw translation units.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is split metadata work; do not infer author intent beyond the explicit new boundaries.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Added a static sdata2_ordering function in ftPp_SpecialHi.c containing float and double literals to influence sdata2 ordering/alignment.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The author called this a hideous hack and asked for a better way; treat as a narrow matching workaround, not accepted style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: The new source uses PAD_STACK in several functions and a custom inline my_sqrtf using __frsqrte and volatile float storage.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded evidence does not include discussion proving these tactics were reviewed as reusable style.

review_feedback.text: please forgive this hideous hack. If there's a better way to align the floats, I'm all ears!

follow_up_queries: bot report 80 new matches 77 broken matches resplit

notes: Strong evidence exists for PR identity, changed files, split/config changes, include updates, and the author comment about sdata2_ordering.
The raw diff loaded for source bodies is truncated, so detailed function-by-function source changes are only partially visible.
The PR body is empty; no author rationale beyond the title and one review comment is available.
Bot report supports match-count and linked-code progress, but the intake did not independently run builds or objdiff.
The only loaded review comment is by the PR author, not an external reviewer; do not infer external reviewer acceptance or requested changes.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Interpret broken matches carefully during TU resplits
accepted_candidate_records.text: During a translation-unit resplit, bot reports can show broken matches in the old unit while listing corresponding new matches in the new units. Compare the broken/new unit paths and split-boundary changes before treating the report as a regression.
evidence_refs: diff:splits.txt SpecialS shortened and SpecialHi/Lw added
text_corpus:80 new matches and 77 broken matches

accepted_candidate_records.title: Resplit source, headers, and object config together
accepted_candidate_records.text: A move-specific resplit should update split metadata, object matching status, new headers, and include users in one coherent patch when declarations move across file boundaries.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat literal-ordering helper functions as hacks requiring evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2466 contains a static sdata2_ordering function used around float/double literals and an author comment calling it a hideous hack for aligning floats. Consider adding or cross-linking guidance that literal/data-order helper functions should be narrow, explicitly justified by verifier evidence, and not treated as normal source style.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already warn against fake data/literal tactics, but this PR provides a concrete historical example of a float-alignment helper being flagged as a hack.

source_update_candidates.title: ftPopo SpecialS range split into SpecialS, SpecialHi, and SpecialLw
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2466, config/GALE01/splits.txt changed ftPp_SpecialS.c to text 0x8011F68C-0x80120E68 and added ftPp_SpecialHi.c text 0x80120E68-0x80122898 plus ftPp_SpecialLw.c text 0x80122898-0x80122EDC, with corresponding sdata2 subranges.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a precise path/split fact supported by the diff, but should be curated against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Old ftPp_1211 include users moved to SpecialHi/SpecialLw headers
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2466 replaces ftPp_1211.h includes in ftCo_0A01.c, ftNn_Init.c, and ftPp_Init.c with ftPp_SpecialHi.h and ftPp_SpecialLw.h.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful include-surface history for curator review; current source should verify the final header layout.

rejection_notes: Do not promote sdata2_ordering as accepted style; the only direct comment calls it a hack.
Do not claim all functions in the new files are independently reviewed or cleanly authored; loaded source diff is truncated and only bot match summaries are available.
Do not infer external reviewer intent or approval from the single loaded review comment, which is authored by lukechampine.
Do not treat the symbol local-label changes as a global naming convention without checking current symbol ownership and data metadata.
